message:
perf(bench): suppress time-travel snapshot clone in perf_update_delete + propagate pragma errors

The perf_update_delete profiler was reporting per-row UPDATE/DELETE
costs that were inflated by an unrelated MemDatabase history clone
running on every explicit COMMIT. This bench never issues FOR SYSTEM_TIME
queries, so the snapshot capture is pure overhead for the workload.

- Hoist the pragma list into a BENCHMARK_PRAGMAS constant and add
  PRAGMA fsqlite_capture_time_travel_snapshots=false; to it, matching
  the suppression already in place for comprehensive_bench's write
  scenarios. This isolates UPDATE/DELETE cost from the time-travel
  capture path.
- Convert apply_benchmark_pragmas from () to Result<(), RunError> and
  propagate errors via ? at the call site. The previous let _ =
  swallowed pragma execution failures, so a typo or unsupported pragma
  would silently fall through and produce results from a misconfigured
  connection — exactly the kind of failure mode that wastes a profile
  run.
- Apply the same Result conversion to the LAB_UNSAFE branch
  (write_merge + ssi_e_process_alpha) so the lab pragmas are equally
  guarded.
- Add benchmark_pragmas_disable_time_travel_capture as a regression
  guard against a future refactor silently dropping the new pragma and
  re-introducing the snapshot-clone overhead in profile output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
